heroHeadline: Day 61: CIA Sovereignty Crisis — Mexico Sends Diplomatic Note Over Unauthorized U.S. Operations; Colombia-US Bust $190M CJNG Crypto Network
heroSubtitle: Day 61 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco (February 22, 2026). DOMINANT DEVELOPMENT: Mexico sent a formal diplomatic note to the U.S. Embassy (April 22) demanding accountability for unauthorized CIA field operations on Mexican territory — two CIA officers and two Mexican officials died in a vehicle crash in the Sierra de Chihuahua on April 20 after destroying six Sinaloa Cartel drug labs without notifying Mexico's federal government. President Sheinbaum stated Mexico 'does not accept foreign operations on its soil'; García Harfuch confirmed the Security Ministry was also not informed. The sovereignty dispute places the bilateral counter-narcotics framework — which underpins the Tapalpa operation and Operativo Enjambre — under its most direct diplomatic pressure since El Mencho's killing. Separately, Colombia and the U.S. announced April 23 the dismantlement of a $190M+ CJNG-linked crypto laundering network (Bogotá/Cali/Jamundí raids, 5 arrests including key cartel liaison 'Dans'/'Johan Alberto Gutiérrez Alonso'), illustrating CJNG's transnational financial architecture remains operational through the succession crisis. FRACTURA TOTAL DAY 13 (declared April 11): The El Jardinero/El Sapo vs. O3/Los Cuinis internal war remains in sustained buildup — no direct inter-faction combat confirmed in the Jalisco heartland through Day 13. Both factions continue territorial positioning. WORLD CUP COUNTDOWN: 48 days to the June 11 FIFA World Cup opening match (Mexico City). Guadalajara's Akron Stadium hosts four group-stage matches; the National Guard surge at all tourist sites (April 22 García Harfuch order) remains in effect. Operativo Enjambre cumulative: 80+ arrests. El 85 sentencing: July 31, 2026.
footerNote: Data sourced from SEDENA, SSPC, FGR, DEA, U.S. DOJ, NCTC, U.S. Treasury (OFAC), InSight Crime, Crisis Group, Wilson Center, Reuters, AP, NPR, Wall Street Journal, El País, Washington Post, CBS News, Al Jazeera, UPI, South China Morning Post, CGTN, France 24, Latin Times, Infobae México, La Silla Rota, WOLA, Milenio, Excélsior, Proceso, El Financiero, El Imparcial, Borderland Beat, RED Michoacán, Semanario ZETA, La Voz de la Frontera, TV Azteca Baja California, Time, CNN, ACLED, OCCRP, El Heraldo de México, and major Mexican news outlets. All contested figures noted. Last update: April 24, 2026.
